title: spergularia rubra subsp. atheniensis
intro: Spergularia rubra subsp. atheniensis, also known as the Athenian sand spurrey, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its reported medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Spergularia rubra subsp. atheniensis typically include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
